Empires & Puzzles Koda Review: The 4-Star Defense Down King?

Koda looks like he walked straight out of an 80s fantasy comic, but does his gameplay hold up? With a crushing -55% defense down, he might just be the budget Titan slayer you've been looking for. We break down if this Bear family brawler is still worth your feeders.
Koda: The Bear-Wearing Barbarian with a Titan-Sized Break
Koda, The Knight of the Plum Forest: He-Man's Goth Cousin
Koda is a 4-star Epic Dark (Purple) hero from the Clash of Knights event, originally released back in late 2023. He belongs to the

, which gives him a unique edge in survivability when buffs are flying around. In a world dominating by flashy modern Construct and Astral heroes, Koda represents a simpler time: hit one guy really hard and make sure they hurt more when everyone else hits them. He is a classic "Glass Cannon" sniper with a utility twist that keeps him relevant in specific niches long after his stats have gathered some dust.
Let's address the elephant—or rather, the bear—in the room. Koda looks like he auditioned for a

reboot but got lost and ended up in a purple forest. He’s rocking a pelt that implies he wrestled a bear and won, face paint that screams "festival season," and a hammer that looks heavy enough to crush a small sedan. The "Knight of the Plum Forest" title feels a bit poetic for a guy who looks like he communicates primarily in grunts and hammer swings. It's a delightfully campy, high-fantasy design that stands out against the more polished, magical aesthetic of newer families.
To put it bluntly, Koda's stats are showing their age.
(794 Power), Koda is significantly behind. He is missing about 100 points of raw power right out of the gate.
This was decent in 2023, but modern 4-star attackers are pushing 800+ easily.
His defense (631) is paper-thin by 2026 standards. Even with Limit Breaks, he struggles to take a hit from modern 5-star snipers.
However, stats aren't everything. Koda's value lies almost entirely in his special skill, which scales independently of his attack stat.
This is Koda's bread and butter.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

Upon release, Koda was seen as a "Titan Specialist" and a solid sniper for 4-star tournaments. He was often compared to Tiburtus and Cheshire Cat.

The game has moved on. Modern 4-stars have stats that rival old 5-stars. Koda cannot survive in a modern 5-star war environment. His damage, while high multiplier-wise, suffers from his low base attack stat compared to the new "Construct" or "Super Elemental" 4-stars.

. If you do not have a premium -60% defense down hero (like some rare 5-star costumes), Koda is likely your best-in-slot for Yellow Titans simply due to that -55% cap. In 4-star Rush or Bloody Battle tournaments, he is still a threat because a -55% defense down usually means the next hit kills.
Koda is a Cleric. You have two schools of thought here, but really only one makes sense for his role.
Maximizes his tile damage against Titans and makes his sniper hit sting more.
He remains squishy and might die in one hit from a strong boss.
Keeps him alive longer to cast his debuff.
Wastes his high damage multiplier and reduces his tile contribution.

path is recommended because Koda is primarily a tool for increasing your Titan scores. You can use items to keep him alive; you can't use items to permanently increase his base attack stat for tile damage.
Reaching -55% defense down is elite, even for 5-stars.
Charges quickly, allowing you to keep the debuff active or refresh it on different targets.
The 5% heal on buff helps offset his squishiness.
Turning defense down ailments into buffs can completely disrupt an opponent's strategy.
Low base defense and health make him very fragile in modern play.
The defense down starts at -35% and takes 2 turns to reach -55%. In fast-paced raids, the target might be dead (or you might be dead) before it maximizes.
He offers zero crowd control. If the board is bad, he can only impact one enemy at a time.
His #1 role. That -55% defense down is huge for high scores against Holy Titans.
His passive heals him when he gets buffs, and he deals solid damage.
Excellent for the early to mid-stages where you need raw defense drops.
Koda provides the physical defense down, Sergei (or C. Domitia/C. Panther) provides the Elemental one. They stack for massive damage.
Leveraging the family bonus to turn incoming defense downs into buffs can surprise attackers who rely on Grimm or Tiburtus.

The community had mixed feelings when Koda dropped.
"I’m wondering the same thing, is this guy useful outside of Titans?"
highlighting his niche nature early on.
Another player praised his Titan utility:
"I had been using Cheshire Cat for titans, but on average my scores went up with Koda... Cat and Koda have bigger drops than Tibs. For titans, take the bigger drop."
And of course, the art critique:
"This guy looks like C.Richard’s high school yearbook photo"
"He looks like he’s come out of some kind of comic book like a He-Man type character."
Koda is a hero out of time—a barbarian with a big hammer in an age of magical constructs and astral elves. While his stats won't impress anyone in 2026, his

remains one of the most potent defense-down tools in the 4-star rarity. He is a specialized tool: you keep him in your toolbox not because he's shiny and new, but because when you need to crack a hard nut (or Titan) open, that hammer never fails.
A Titan essential for mid-game players and a respectable depth hero for veterans. Not quite a "must-limit-break," but definitely a "must-keep."
Is Koda still worth leveling in 2026? We analyze his massive -55% defense down, Bear family bonus, and viability against modern Titans and in 4-star tournaments.
